content.text:
All three findings are **COUNCIL_VERIFIED** with an intact integrity chain (F-003 re-hash matches). Investigation complete.

---

## Investigative narrative — Case ROCBA-MEM (Stark Research Labs IP-theft)

…[1 line(s) of official-evidence tool-output rows redacted]

**What the evidence does and does not support:** the verified findings establish the OS/timeline, an exposed RDP service, and external RDP sessions reaching the host — but `netscan` proves only the TCP sessions and their endpoints; it does **not** by itself prove RDP authentication succeeded (deliberately downgraded to MEDIUM and stated in the finding, which is why the inference seat passed). On the **data-theft/staging** angle I found **no supporting evidence**: `windows.cmdline` and process paths showed **no archiving/exfil/remote tooling** (no rclone, WinRAR/7-Zip, robocopy, FTP/SFTP, PsExec, AnyDesk/TeamViewer, ngrok, PowerShell downloaders) and **no processes running from Temp/Downloads/Public/ProgramData** staging directories — so I filed no exfil claim rather than speculate. **Recommended next steps** to close the loop: pull the security event log / RDP logon artifacts (e.g. registry + `windows.registry`, EVTX `4624/4625` Logon Type 10) to confirm whether any of the four external sessions authenticated, and resolve/attribute `81.30.144.115` and `213.202.233.104`.
